demographics.audit_notes: Data for this period is highly speculative as the area was an IBM campus with minimal residential population. Tract 308.0 did not exist; data is for the geographic area. New fields are estimated based on proxy parent tracts.
Values are interpolated between 1990 and 2000 census data points for proxy tracts. Confidence remains low due to the area's non-residential nature at the time.
Area was still pre-development. Population values are plausible but hard to verify precisely. Tract 308.0 did not exist.

This year marks the beginning of residential development. Population jump is plausible. Data is interpolated between 2000 and 2010 census points, adjusted for known development timelines. First ACS data becomes available for parent tracts.
Population growth reflects the opening of the first major residential and retail phases of The Domain. Values are plausible interpolations.
Values align well with 2010 Census and ACS 2006-2010 data for the parent tract (18.16) which contained the Domain area. The demographic shift is evident.
Continued rapid growth is consistent with ACS 5-year estimates for the parent tract. The population more than doubled in 5 years.
Corrected values to align with official 2020 Census and ACS 5-Year data for the newly created Tract 308.0. Original values were very close.
Post-2020 data are projections based on recent ACS trends and known construction pipeline. The growth rate is plausible for this high-demand area.
Projected values continuing the trend from recent ACS data and development activity.
Projected values continuing the trend from recent ACS data and development activity.

property.audit_notes: Property data reflects the area as a large, single-use corporate campus (IBM). No residential or retail market existed.
Values are stable, reflecting the pre-redevelopment period.
Slight decrease in office space and increase in vacancy may reflect the dot-com bust and IBM's plans to sell the land.
Massive shift in property use as redevelopment begins. Office space plummets as old IBM buildings are demolished; retail space appears as Phase 1 is built. Permit data shows start of activity.
The Domain officially opens. First residential and retail data appears. All values show sharp increases, reflecting the 'instant neighborhood' creation.
Values align with market data for this period. Strong growth continues post-launch.
Office space nearly doubles as major tech tenants move in. All metrics show continued high growth and demand.
Data aligns with market sources for Tract 308.0. Vacancy rate ticks up slightly, possibly due to pandemic effects and new supply coming online.
Projections reflect continued development but also a softening in the commercial real estate market, with higher vacancy rates.
Projected values based on market trends and construction pipeline.
Projected values based on market trends and construction pipeline.

socioeconomic.audit_notes: CORRECTED: Original median income of $85k was implausibly high for 1990. Replaced with an estimate based on parent tract census data, with a premium for tech employment. With a tiny residential population, this metric is not very meaningful.
CORRECTED: Income interpolated based on corrected 1990 and 2000 values.
CORRECTED: Original income of $98.5k was too high. Replaced with value based on 2000 Census for parent tract.

Socioeconomic profile begins to shift dramatically with new development. Income rises with first residents; job mix diversifies with retail, lowering tech %. New fields estimated from first ACS releases.
Number of businesses explodes with retail opening. Income reflects new high-end apartment dwellers.

CORRECTED: Adjusted income to be more in line with ACS data for the parent tract, which is higher than the surrounding area but not as high as originally stated. Gini coefficient is high, reflecting mix of high-income residents and lower-wage service jobs.
CORRECTED: Adjusted income to align with ACS data and create a smoother trend. Tech job percentage rebounds as new office towers attract tech companies.
CORRECTED: Original income of $165k was a significant outlier compared to ACS 5-year data for Tract 308.0 (approx. $122k). Adjusted to a value closer to the authoritative source.
CORRECTED: Projected income adjusted downward to follow the corrected 2020 baseline. Other values are plausible extrapolations.
CORRECTED: Projected income adjusted downward to follow the corrected 2020 baseline.
CORRECTED: Projected income adjusted downward to follow the corrected 2020 baseline.
